findings.claim: Livsmedelsverket rekommenderar D-vitamintillskott 20 µg/dag för alla över 75 år
findings.source: Livsmedelsverket, Kostråd för vuxna över 65 år (2026), L 2026 nr 07

findings.claim: Äldre över 65 år har ett ökat proteinbehov för att bibehålla muskelstyrka
findings.source: Livsmedelsverket (2026); EFSA DRV for protein (0.83 g/kg/dag)

findings.claim: Multivitamin supplementation significantly improved delayed free recall in adults 65+ (SMD = 0.09, p < 0.0001)
findings.source: Xu et al., Food Science and Human Wellness (2024). Systematic review, 10 RCTs, n=13,600

findings.claim: Vitamin D, probiotics, and PUFAs (omega-3) likely reduce cognitive decline risk in older adults; vitamins A, B, C, E showed no significant effects
findings.source: PMC systematic review (2024). 33 primary studies in adults 65+

findings.claim: Multicomponent exercise + nutrition supplements reduced frailty risk by relative change 28% in older adults (low certainty)
findings.source: BMC Geriatrics systematic review (2024). 7 nutritional intervention RCTs, n=258

findings.claim: Protein intake of 1.2 g/kg/day recommended for adults 65+ (vs 0.83 g/kg for younger adults)
findings.source: Dorrington N., Review of nutritional requirements for adults ≥65 years in the UK (2020). SACN review.

findings.claim: Over 90% of older adults in Europe have vitamin D intakes below 10 µg/day
findings.source: Proceedings of the Nutrition Society (2019). Nutritional challenges for older adults in Europe.

findings.claim: Vitamin B12 deficiency common in older adults due to decreased absorption (atrophic gastritis, PPI use)
findings.source: FSAI, Scientific Recommendations for Food-Based Dietary Guidelines for Older Adults in Ireland (2021)

findings.claim: Calcium recommendation 1000 mg/day for adults 65+; calcium + vitamin D combined may reduce fracture risk
findings.source: Dorrington N. (2020); EFSA DRV for calcium

findings.claim: B vitamin supplementation (B12, folate, B6) improves cognition in those with mild cognitive impairment but not in healthy older adults without deficiency
findings.source: Berg et al., Nutrition Reviews (2025). Systematic review and meta-analysis.

findings.claim: Micronutrient supplementation in frail elderly: evidence quality is low to very low for most outcomes
findings.source: Paraskevas et al., Healthcare (2025). Systematic review of 11 RCTs.

findings.claim: EFSA tolerable upper intake level for vitamin D: 100 µg/day for adults
findings.source: EFSA DRV update (2016-2019). Tolerable Upper Intake Level summary.

findings.claim: Oral nutrition supplements significantly increase energy intake, body weight, BMI, and grip strength in community-dwelling older adults
findings.source: BMC Nutrition (2025). Systematic review, 12 RCTs, n=1320.

findings.claim: NNR 2023 and Livsmedelsverket 2025 generella kostråd: basis for elderly recommendations; higher protein and vitamin D are key differences
54% of Boomers worldwide take multivitamins or single vitamin/mineral supplements for healthy aging
findings.source: Innova Market Insights, Senior Nutrition Trends (2026)

summary: Strong Tier 1 evidence supports D-vitamin supplementation (20 µg/day from 75 år), increased protein intake (1.2 g/kg/day recommended), and multivitamin use for cognitive function (delayed recall). Evidence for vitamin D in frailty is mixed but overall supportive. B12 and calcium are common deficiencies in older adults. Livsmedelsverket published new official guidelines in 2026.
